question: With no per-worker overrides, what is the default value of `camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout` — the time a job remains exclusively assigned to a worker?

a.text: The properties reference for `camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout` ("The time a job remains exclusively assigned to the worker.") gives a default of `PT5M`.

b.text: `PT0.1S` is the default `poll-interval`, not the job lock timeout.

c.text: `PT10S` is `camunda.client.request-timeout`, unrelated to how long a job remains locked to a worker.

d.text: `PT1H` is the default `camunda.client.message-time-to-live`, not the worker job timeout.

explanation: `camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout` controls how long an activated job stays exclusively locked to the worker, and its default is `PT5M`. Poll interval (`PT0.1S`), request timeout (`PT10S`) and message TTL (`PT1H`) are separate properties.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
